name: minimal
description: Bare-bones docs: just index.md (overview + run) and architecture.md. Good for small projects.
structure:
```text
mkdocs.yml                     <- MkDocs config (see the mkdocs.yml template)
docs/
  index.md                     <- Project overview + how to run
  architecture.md              <- High-level architecture with one diagram
.magestic-ai/.docgen.json      <- {last_run, head_sha, scope_hash}
```
mkdocs_yml:
```yaml
site_name: <project name from package.json/pyproject.toml or directory name>
site_description: <one-line from README or CLAUDE.md>
docs_dir: docs

theme:
  name: material
  features:
    - navigation.top
    - search.suggest
    - search.highlight
    - content.code.copy
  palette:
    - media: "(prefers-color-scheme: light)"
      scheme: default
      toggle:
        icon: material/weather-sunny
        name: Switch to dark mode
    - media: "(prefers-color-scheme: dark)"
      scheme: slate
      toggle:
        icon: material/weather-night
        name: Switch to light mode

markdown_extensions:
  - admonition
  - pymdownx.superfences:
      custom_fences:
        - name: mermaid
          class: mermaid
          format: !!python/name:pymdownx.superfences.fence_code_format
  - tables
  - toc:
      permalink: true

nav:
  - Home: index.md
  - Architecture: architecture.md
```
page_templates:
### `docs/index.md`

Keep it tight. Cover:

- One-paragraph "what is this" from CLAUDE.md / README
- How to install and run it locally (the essential commands)
- Where to go next (link to architecture.md)

End with the `docgen` marker:

```markdown
<!-- docgen:auto -->
*Auto-generated. Last updated: <YYYY-MM-DD> from commit `<short-sha>`.*
```

### `docs/architecture.md`

- One-paragraph elevator pitch of how the system fits together.
- A single Mermaid diagram of the major components:

   ````markdown
   ```mermaid
   graph TD
       A[Entry Point] --> B[Core]
       B --> C[(Data)]
   ```
   ````

- A short table of top-level directories with one-line purposes.

Pull facts only from files you actually read. Don't invent. Do NOT create
`api/` or `guides/` pages for this template - two pages is the whole site.
extra_instructions: Keep the site to exactly two pages. Do not create api/ or guides/ subtrees.
